title: gymnogramma tartarea
intro: Gymnogramma tartarea, also known as False Daisy or Gymnogramma, is a flowering plant species in the family Asteraceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its chemical compounds such as sesquiterpenes and flavonoids. The health benefits of Gymnogramma tartarea include its ability to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. Herbal preparations of Gymnogramma tartarea can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water or using them in a decoction, and it is also used in some traditional remedies in the form of a tea or tincture.
